title: ～に関わって
titleEn: Indicates that something is related to or influenced by a certain matter or factor; 'concerning', 'in connection with', 'regarding'.
structure: Verb-casual + に関わって, い-Adjective + に関わって, な-Adjective + に関わって, Noun + に関わって
structureEn: Verb-casual + に関わって, い-Adjective + に関わって, な-Adjective + に関わって, Noun + に関わって
explanation: Indicates that something is related to or influenced by a certain matter or factor; 'concerning', 'in connection with', 'regarding'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に関わって grammar point is used to express that something is related to, influenced by, or connected with a certain matter or factor. It can be translated as 'concerning', 'in connection with', or 'regarding' in English. The formation depends on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

title: ～に限り
explanation: Expresses limitation; 'only', 'limited to', 'exclusive to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に限り grammar point is used to indicate that something is limited or exclusive to a certain condition or situation. It can be translated as 'only', 'limited to', or 'exclusive to' in English. The formation is simple and can be used with nouns.

title: ～に際して
titleEn: Used to express the moment or the occasion of an action; 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when'.
explanation: Used to express the moment or the occasion of an action; 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～に際して grammar point describes an action that occurs during a specific event, occasion, or when something takes place. It can be translated into English as 'at the time of', 'upon', 'when', or 'in the event of'. This grammar is used with nouns to describe the context or situation during which an action takes place.

title: ～ねばならない
titleEn: Expresses obligation or necessity; 'must', 'have to', 'need to'.
structure: Verb-ない form → (remove い) → ね + ばならない
structureEn: Verb-ない form → (remove い) → ね + ばならない
explanation: Expresses obligation or necessity; 'must', 'have to', 'need to'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～ねばならない grammar point is used to express obligation or necessity. It can be translated as 'must', 'have to', or 'need to' in English. This grammar point is mainly used with verbs, indicating that the action must be done.

title: ～のみならず～も
titleEn: Not only... but also; as well as.
structure: Verb-casual + のみならず + (も), い-Adjective + のみならず + (も), な-Adjective + のみならず + (も), Noun + のみならず + (も)
structureEn: Verb-casual + のみならず + (も), い-Adjective + のみならず + (も), な-Adjective + のみならず + (も), Noun + のみならず + (も)
explanation: Not only... but also; as well as. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～のみならず～も grammar point is used to show that something applies not only to one thing, but also to another thing. This can be translated as 'not only... but also', or 'as well as' in English. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

title: ～のももっともだ
titleEn: Express that something is understandable or reasonable.
structure: Verb-casual + のももっともだ, い-Adjective + のももっともだ, な-Adjective + なのももっともだ, Noun + なのももっともだ
structureEn: Verb-casual + のももっともだ, い-Adjective + のももっともだ, な-Adjective + なのももっともだ, Noun + なのももっともだ
explanation: Express that something is understandable or reasonable. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～のももっともだ grammar point is used to express that a situation, action, or feeling is understandable or reasonable, considering the circumstances. The formation differs depending on whether it is used with a verb, い-adjective, な-adjective, or noun.

title: 〜の上では
titleEn: Indicates 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'.
structure: Noun or Phrase + の上では
structureEn: Noun or Phrase + の上では
explanation: Indicates 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The 〜の上では grammar point is used to show that you are expressing a view or making a judgment based on a specific standpoint or perspective. It can be translated as 'on the basis of', 'in terms of', or 'from the standpoint of'. The formation is straightforward, which involves attaching 〜の上では to a noun or a phrase.

title: ～の下で
titleEn: Under the context or condition of; under.
structure: Verb-casual + の下で, い-Adjective + の下で, な-Adjective + の下で, Noun + の下で
structureEn: Verb-casual + の下で, い-Adjective + の下で, な-Adjective + の下で, Noun + の下で
explanation: Under the context or condition of; under. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～の下で grammar point is used to express that something occurs under a certain context, condition, or circumstance. It can be translated as 'under' or 'in' in English. The formation is simple, as it can be used with verbs, い-adjectives, な-adjectives, and nouns.
